title: ADT Message Routing Failures
spl:
index=interfaces sourcetype="hl7:v2" msg_type="ADT*"
| search routing_status="FAIL" OR ErrorCode!="0"
| stats count, values(pid) as patients by sending_facility, receiving_system
| sort -count
description: ADT misroutes leave wrong census in downstream systems (lab, HIE), affecting care and billing.
value: ADT misroutes leave wrong census in downstream systems (lab, HIE), affecting care and billing.
implementation: Reconciliation job for ADT A03 without matching A01; monitor duplicate PV1 segments.
visualization: Table (failed routes), Sankey (facility → system), Line chart (failure trend).
knownFalsePositives: ADT routing noise during go-live cutovers, sub-system upgrades, and bulk patient transfers between facilities; use interface backlogs, not just counts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Interface engine.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="hl7:v2"`, `msg_type="ADT"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Reconciliation job for ADT A03 without matching A01; monitor duplicate PV1 segments.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=interfaces sourcetype="hl7:v2" msg_type="ADT*"
| search routing_status="FAIL" OR ErrorCode!="0"
| stats count, values(pid) as patients by sending_facility, receiving_system
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**ADT Message Routing Failures** — ADT misroutes leave wrong census in downstream systems (lab, HIE), affecting care and billing.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="hl7:v2"`, `msg_type="ADT"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Interface engine. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: interfaces; **sourcetype**: hl7:v2. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=interfaces, sourcetype="hl7:v2". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sending_facility, receiving_system** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed routes), Sankey (facility → system), Line chart (failure trend).
grandmaExplanation: In Healthcare, we pay close attention to "ADT Message Routing Failures" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
